questions.question: In triangle ABC, side BC = 6.5 cm, side AC = 8.4 cm and angle C = 20°. Calculate the length of the missing side AB to 3 significant figures.
questions.explanation: Using the cosine rule c² = a² + b² - 2ab cos C: AB² = 6.5² + 8.4² - 2(6.5)(8.4)cos(20°) ≈ 10.20, so AB ≈ 3.19 cm.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, side AB = 2 cm, side AC = 1 cm and angle A = 60°. Calculate the length of side BC.
In triangle ABC, side AB = 4 cm, side BC = 8 cm and side AC = 10 cm. Find the size of angle ABC to 1 decimal place.
A plane flies from airport A on a bearing of 040° for 120 km and then on a bearing of 130° for 150 km. Calculate the distance of the plane from the airport to the nearest km.
questions.explanation: The change in direction from 040° to 130° is 90°, forming a right-angled triangle. Distance = √(120² + 150²) = √36900 ≈ 192 km.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, a = 8 cm, angle A = 30° and angle B = 72°. Find the length of side b.
questions.explanation: Using the sine rule b / sin B = a / sin A: b = (8 × sin 72°) / sin 30° ≈ 15.2 cm.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, side a = 6.2 cm, side b = 4.5 cm and angle A = 80°. Calculate the size of angle B.
In triangle ABC, BC = 6 cm, AC = 4.5 cm and angle ABC = 45°. Calculate the two possible values of angle BAC.
Calculate the area of a triangle with sides 8.6 cm and 7.8 cm and an included angle of 45°.
questions.explanation: Area = ½ab sin C = 0.5 × 8.6 × 7.8 × sin 45° ≈ 23.7 cm².

question_types.title: Area of Triangles 9D — Finding Unknown Angles

questions.question: In triangle ABC, side AB = 5 cm, side BC = 6 cm and area = 12 cm². Given that AC is the longest side, find angle B.
questions.explanation: ½ × 5 × 6 × sin B = 12 → sin B = 0.8 → B = 53.1° or 127°. Since AC is the longest side, angle B must be obtuse: B = 127°.

question_types.title: Solving Triangle Problems 9E — Navigation and Distances

questions.question: A hiker walks due north from A for 8 km to B, then on a bearing of 120° for 8 km to C. Find the distance AC.
questions.explanation: Bearing 120° means angle ABC (interior) = 180° - 120° = 60°. With AB = BC = 8 km and included angle 60°, triangle ABC is equilateral. AC = 8 km.

questions.question: Identify the period of the graph y = tan x.
questions.explanation: The tangent function repeats every 180° (or π radians).

questions.question: Describe the transformation from y = sin x to y = sin(2x).
questions.explanation: Replacing x with 2x compresses the graph horizontally — a horizontal stretch by scale factor 1/2.
